entities.name: vākya
entities.cite: while the sentence is being manifested by the causes of its manifestation

entities.name: buddhi
entities.cite: cognition first proceeds in the form of a seizing of parts

entities.name: bhāgāvagraha
entities.cite: the hearer's buddhi runs ahead in bhaga-avagraha-rupa, the form of part-seizure

entities.name: sphota
entities.cite: when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

entities.name: bhāga
entities.cite: it takes words, phrases, apparent constituents, as so many determinate grasps

entities.name: avagraha
entities.cite: the form of part-seizure — it takes words, phrases, apparent constituents

axioms.name: pravartate
axioms.cite: cognition first proceeds in the form of a seizing of parts

axioms.name: anyatha
axioms.cite: These part-graspings are the anyatha-determinations of 1.89: committed, useful, and destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

axioms.name: paurvapravartana
axioms.cite: cognition first proceeds in the form of a seizing of parts

denials.name: nitya
denials.cite: destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

claims.name: pravartate
claims.cite: cognition first proceeds in the form of a seizing of parts

claims.name: anyatha
claims.cite: committed, useful, and destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole

rejected_readings.label: direct_whole_grasp
rejected_readings.rendering: Cognition directly grasps the undivided meaning without first proceeding through part-seizure.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the verse's explicit statement that cognition proceeds 'first' (pūrvaṃ) in the form of part-seizure. The commentary emphasizes the necessary intermediate stage of part-graspings before whole apprehension.

rejected_readings.label: permanent_parts
rejected_readings.rendering: The part-seizures constitute the final and permanent grasp of meaning.
rejected_readings.why: Directly contradicts the commentary's explicit statement that part-graspings are 'destined to be superseded when the vakya-sphota is seized whole'. Part-grasps are provisional scaffolding, not endpoint.

rejected_readings.label: sentence_as_object
rejected_readings.rendering: The sentence (vākya) itself is the final object of comprehension, not the undivided sphota.
rejected_readings.why: Confuses the audible manifestation (vākya) with its meaning-whole (sphota). The commentary makes clear that the sentence is the medium through which part-grasps arise, but the sphota is the true endpoint, retrospectively conferring meaning on the intermediate words.

notes: Verse 1.90 encodes the phenomenology of sentential comprehension: part-seizure (bhāgāvagraha) is the cognitive mode through which the hearer apprehends a sentence, but this mode is provisional and destined to collapse into whole apprehension of sphota. The six sorts fit well: buddhi (cognition), bhāgāvagraha (power), sphota (absolute), vakya (manifestation), bhāga and avagraha (properties/powers of the cognitive process).
